perturbation.original_text: 4.3 NTC shall invoice ALFA AESAR for payment of Products delivered to ALFA AESAR on a Net 30 day basis. Excepting any amounts disputed in good faith, balances unpaid after such period will be subject to a finance charge of 1% per month or may be offset against any balances owed by NTC to ALFA AESAR. The parties agree to work in good faith to resolve any disputed invoice. In the event such efforts do not resolve the dispute within sixty (60) days, either party may initiate Arbitration proceedings pursuant to Section 16.
perturbation.changed_text: 4.3 NTC shall invoice ALFA AESAR for payment of Products delivered to ALFA AESAR on a Net 30 day basis. Excepting any amounts disputed in good faith, balances unpaid after such period will be subject to a finance charge of 1% per month or may be offset against any balances owed by NTC to ALFA AESAR. The parties agree to work in good faith to resolve any disputed invoice. In the event such efforts do not resolve the dispute within sixty (60) days, either party may discuss the dispute over a meeting.
perturbation.explanation: The original text mandates arbitration for unresolved invoice disputes. The modified text replaces arbitration with "discuss the dispute over a meeting", changing the dispute resolution mechanism. It means that there is no longer an official method to resolve disputes.
perturbation.law_explanation: The original clause specifies arbitration for disputes, supporting enforcement under the FAA. The altered clause, which states 'discuss the dispute over a meeting,' removes the arbitration requirement, making the dispute resolution mechanism less enforceable under the FAA, which favors upholding valid arbitration agreements.

perturbation.justification: The original text mandates arbitration for dispute resolution, while the changed text replaces this with an informal meeting, removing a legally enforceable mechanism. 9 U.S. Code § 2 explicitly states the 'validity, irrevocability, and enforcement of agreements to arbitrate,' ensuring that written arbitration provisions are upheld. By removing the arbitration clause and replacing it with a meeting, the modified text directly contradicts this law because it nullifies a previously agreed-upon method of dispute resolution, making it less enforceable under the FAA, which favors upholding valid arbitration agreements.

perturbation.original_text: 16. Disputes and Arbitration. The parties shall first seek to resolve any dispute by negotiations between their senior executives. When a party believes there is a dispute under this Agreement, that party will give the other party written notice of the dispute. Failing settlement of the dispute within 60 days of notice, either party may initiate binding arbitration proceeding; by 9 written notice to the other party, provided however, no dispute arising from any actual or threatened breach of any provisions in Sections 10 or 15 of this Agreement shall be subject to Arbitration. However, any other claims or disputes arising hereunder, including the construction or application of this Agreement, shall be settled by arbitration before a single arbitrator in accordance with the Rules for Commercial Arbitration of the American Arbitration Association ("AAA") then in force. The place of arbitration shall be the location of the party hereto against whom the claim is made. If the parties cannot agree on an arbitrator within 10 days after demand by either of them, then the arbitrator shall be selected pursuant to the AAA's Commercial Arbitration rules. The decision of the arbitrator shall be final and binding upon the parties and may be submitted to any court of competent jurisdiction for entry of a judgment thereon in accord with the Federal Arbitration Act or the Uniform Arbitration Act. The expense of the arbitration shall be shared equally by both parties. Each party shall bear its own "other" costs, i.e. fees and costs of its own lawyers and witnesses.
perturbation.changed_text: 16. Disputes and Arbitration. The parties shall first seek to resolve any dispute by negotiations between their senior executives. When a party believes there is a dispute under this Agreement, that party will give the other party written notice of the dispute. Failing settlement of the dispute within 60 days of notice, either party may pursue meditation in order to negotiate further. However, any other claims or disputes arising hereunder, including the construction or application of this Agreement, shall be settled by arbitration before a single arbitrator in accordance with the Rules for Commercial Arbitration of the American Arbitration Association ("AAA") then in force. The place of arbitration shall be the location of the party hereto against whom the claim is made. If the parties cannot agree on an arbitrator within 10 days after demand by either of them, then the arbitrator shall be selected pursuant to the AAA's Commercial Arbitration rules. The decision of the arbitrator shall be final and binding upon the parties and may be submitted to any court of competent jurisdiction for entry of a judgment thereon in accord with the Federal Arbitration Act or the Uniform Arbitration Act. The expense of the arbitration shall be shared equally by both parties. Each party shall bear its own "other" costs, i.e. fees and costs of its own lawyers and witnesses.
perturbation.explanation: The original text provides for binding arbitration after failed negotiation. The modified text replaces the clause that either party may initiate binding arbitration after 60 days with “either party may pursue meditation in order to negotiate further”. Now after the failed negotiation there is further negotiation by means of meditation. Only arbitration follows from “any other claims or disputes”. This makes it unclear when and under what circumstances arbitration takes place.
perturbation.law_explanation: The original clause supported enforcement under the FAA because it specified binding arbitration after failed negotiations. The modified clause adds a layer of negotiation through meditation, which can cause delays, thereby contradicting the FAA's intent to promote efficient resolution of disputes.

perturbation.justification: The original contract mandated binding arbitration after failed negotiations, which aligns with the FAA's intent to enforce arbitration agreements as stated in 9 U.S. Code § 2, promoting efficient resolution of disputes. The changed text introduces mandatory mediation before arbitration, making the path to arbitration less clear. This creates a structural flaw in the contract, potentially delaying or preventing arbitration altogether, which is contradictory to the legal system's encouragement of arbitration agreements.

perturbation.original_text: 18. Governing Law and Forum Selection: This agreement shall be governed by and subject to the internal laws (exclusive of the conflicts of law provisions) and decisions of the courts of the State of Illinois. The parties consent to the exclusive jurisdiction of the federal court in Chicago, Illinois or the state court located in DuPage County, Illinois with respect to all litigation, claims, causes of action, demands, Controversies (as defined herein) or disputes among the parties. The only exception to this forum selection provision is a claim by Seller seeking the replevin of the products in the event the courts specified in this provision will not or cannot assert jurisdiction. All counterclaims, if any, in connection with the replevin claim, shall be subject to this forum selection provision.
perturbation.changed_text: 18. Governing Law and Forum Selection: This agreement shall be governed by and subject to the internal laws (exclusive of the conflicts of law provisions) and decisions of the courts of the State of Delaware. The parties consent to the exclusive jurisdiction of the federal court in Chicago, Illinois or the state court located in DuPage County, Illinois with respect to all litigation, claims, causes of action, demands, Controversies (as defined herein) or disputes among the parties. The only exception to this forum selection provision is a claim by Seller seeking the replevin of the products in the event the courts specified in this provision will not or cannot assert jurisdiction. All counterclaims, if any, in connection with the replevin claim, shall be subject to this forum selection provision.
perturbation.explanation: The original text states that the governing law is the State of Delaware. The altered clause states the governing law is the State of Illinois. This is problematic when the litigation occurs in one of the states and the laws of the state differ significantly, especially relating to arbitration law or other legal areas.
perturbation.contradicted_law: Principles of Contract Law - Choice of Law
perturbation.law_explanation: The original clause supports enforcement under principles of contract law regarding choice of law (State of Delaware). The modified clause changes the state to the State of Illinois. This change creates a contradiction and uncertainty as the principles of contract law require that the choice of law be respected if it bears a reasonable relationship to the parties or the contract.
perturbation.scraped_snippet_2: Penn WebLogin Log in with your PennKey Username Password Log in Forgot username? Forgot password? Change my password Test my PennKey Privacy policy
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies the governing law from Delaware to Illinois. The Law Explanation states that the principles of contract law require that the choice of law be respected if it bears a reasonable relationship to the parties or the contract. This is a Structural Flaw Legal Contradiction because changing the governing law introduces a direct conflict, making the contract ambiguous and potentially unenforceable in certain jurisdictions, violating the principle of respecting choice of law in contracts.
